_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: Keypad Password Lock
subtitle: Secure Access with a 4-Digit PIN!
overview: Students build a simple password-protected door lock system using Arduino, a 4x4 keypad, a servo motor, LEDs, and a buzzer. When the correct 4-digit PIN is entered, the green LED glows and the servo unlocks the door. Incorrect PIN entries activate the red LED and buzzer alarm.

images._note: Add photos of keypad connections, servo lock mechanism, LEDs, buzzer, and student-built security systems.

gallery.caption: Servo Motor used as a door lock mechanism
Buzzer alarm for incorrect PIN attempts
Complete Arduino Keypad Password Lock Project

keyTopics: Servo Motor as an Electronic Door Lock

youtubeVideos.title: Servo Motor with Arduino
youtubeVideos.desc: Control a servo motor for locking and unlocking mechanisms.

activity.title: Build a Keypad Password Lock
steps: Connect the 4x4 keypad to Arduino digital pins
Connect the servo motor to a PWM pin
Connect green and red LEDs with resistors
Connect the buzzer to a digital output pin
Enter the correct 4-digit PIN using the keypad
Observe the green LED turning ON
Observe the servo motor rotating to unlock
Observe the red LED and buzzer alarm activation

quiz.q: What device is used to enter the password?
quiz.explanation: The keypad is used to enter the 4-digit PIN.

quiz.q: How many digits are used in the PIN for this project?
What happens when the correct PIN is entered?
options: Green LED glows and servo unlocks
quiz.explanation: A correct PIN unlocks the system and turns on the green LED.

quiz.q: What happens when the wrong PIN is entered?
quiz.explanation: Incorrect passwords trigger an alert using the red LED and buzzer.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of the servo motor?
quiz.explanation: The servo rotates to lock or unlock the door.

quiz.q: Which component provides an audible alarm?
quiz.explanation: The buzzer produces sound when a wrong password is entered.

quiz.q: What color LED indicates successful access?
quiz.explanation: Green commonly represents success or access granted.

quiz.q: Which Arduino concept is mainly used to check passwords?
quiz.explanation: The program compares entered values using conditions.

quiz.q: Where can similar systems be found?
quiz.explanation: Password locks are commonly used for doors and restricted access areas.

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of this project?
quiz.explanation: The project demonstrates electronic security using password authentication.
